title: althaea bertramii
intro: Althaea bertramii, also known as marshmallow, is a perennial herb native to Europe and North Africa, belonging to the mallow family. Its roots and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Althaea bertramii is rich in mucilages, which provide a protective barrier to the mucous membranes, making it an effective remedy for digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or syrup, and is also used in topical creams and ointments to alleviate symptoms of eczema, wounds, and gastrointestinal disorders.
